raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "category": "relational_attention",
      "description": "The human-authored Bill of Rights (BoR) is entirely undefined. All six articles (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) are placeholders awaiting human principal authorship. This leaves the DIO without any constitutional perimeter, human-centric constraints, or definition of the human principal’s non-negotiable boundaries.",
      "grounding": "BoR Articles I–VI, each marked '[To be defined by human principal]', and the MSP section 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' which states the MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR and that Arbiter verifies the BoR perimeter.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Systemic. Arbiter cannot enforce any scope; MSP default-permissive mode persists indefinitely. No action proposal can be meaningfully checked against the human’s constitutional boundaries, risking unbounded operations that may violate eventual human constraints. The entire governance architecture (Nomos gate, PEM conflict handling, scope expansion) is inoperable without a populated BoR.",
      "related_msp_section": "Relationship to the Bill of Rights, Amendment Protocol (scope expansion requires BoR amendment)"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "category": "relational_attention",
      "description": "The human-principal notification channel (mailbox) required for MSP and BoR amendment workflows is not registered in the Minder peer registry. The MSP Amendment Protocol states: 'an HOM notification is emitted to the `human-principal` mailbox.' However, the current Minder slice shows only a generic peer entry for Leon with no associated notification endpoint or mailbox configuration. Without this, any PEM that triggers a BoR or MSP amendment cannot reach the human for the required decision.",
      "grounding": "MSP Amendment Protocol: 'The draft enters a review state and an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox. Activation requires a POST /amend/review with decision=approve.' Minder peer record for Leon lacks any 'mailbox' or 'notification_preference' field.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Process-blocking. The DIO cannot execute the constitutionally defined amendment pipeline. Any PEM that classifies a conflict as BOR_CONFLICT or requires an MSP amendment will stall indefinitely, preventing governance adaptation. The human’s relational attention cannot be solicited at the moment it is most needed.",
      "related_msp_section": "Amendment Protocol"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "category": "relational_attention",
      "description": "No formal peer commitment records exist in the Minder registry or the constitutional documents. The BoR is expected to define the human authority reservation and the boundaries of the relationship, but even aside from the BoR, there are no recorded commitments that map human non-negotiable principles (abundantly captured in deductive observations) into binding agreements acknowledged by the DIO. The observations about Leon’s architectural and governance requirements (e.g., immutability, dual-write, IP protection) are not formalized into commitments that Nomos can enforce.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) and the Minder recent observations, which contain rich inferred constraints but no corresponding `peer_commitment` entries linking those constraints to the person-attention model. The MSP’s relationship to the BoR implies that the BoR is the formal commitment artifact; its absence means commitments are absent.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Operational ambiguity. Inferred constraints (observations) remain advisory; Nomos cannot use them at the compliance gate because they are not part of the BoR perimeter or MSP rules. This undermines the human’s expectation that his stated principles will be respected. Commitments are the relational glue that turn observations into enforceable boundaries.",
      "related_msp_section": "Relationship to the Bill of Rights, Rules (MSP currently empty), Genome References"
    }
  ]
}
